frameworks.name: theory-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: strong model-based RL where the model is an intuitive causal theory of the environment

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: generalized by replacing the internal model with a structured causal theory

frameworks.name: intuitive theory / object-based causal model
frameworks.one_line: ontology of physical objects, intentional agents, relations, and goals

frameworks.name: alternative learning models
frameworks.one_line: non-theory-based baselines compared on fit to prefrontal activity

core_question: How are intuitive theories of the environment represented and updated in the human brain during reinforcement learning?
key_assumptions: internal models are structured as intuitive causal theories
theories decompose into separable components (objects, agents, relations, goals)
theory updating produces distinct neural signatures across components
manipulations: participants learned multiple Atari-style video games during fMRI
theory-based RL model contrasted against alternative learning models
theory update events identified across learning
key_findings: inferred theories explained inferior frontal gyrus and prefrontal signal better than alternatives
theory update events elicited activity in IFG, occipital, and fusiform cortex
separate learning signals indexed different theory components
transient strengthening of theory representations during updating
interpretations: intuitive theories are neurally represented across prefrontal and posterior regions
effective connectivity flows top-down from prefrontal theory-coding regions to occipital/temporal updating regions
